GameSource/Unity/../Game/BrnLoadingScree.notes: Complex/interpretive. Built the CgsGraphics Im2d render subsystem (ImRendererBase/ImRenderer<V>/Im2dBase<V>/Im2d + Im2dTransform + Basic2dColouredTexturedVertex) and a minimal renderengine::Texture/Texture2D API, then reconstructed all 5 funcs. FAITHFUL: AddCommand (command->state switch), RenderBackground/RenderForeground (timestep + visibility logic), and the black-overlay fade-in/out control flow + Im2d draw sequence (Begin/SetTransform/SetState/SetTexture/Render/End). INTERPRETIVE (documented inline): RenderBlackOverlay's quad geometry/transform was hand-written PPC VMX that Hex-Rays failed to lift ('local variable allocation has failed') -> rewritten as the equivalent clean logical full-screen quad (per the gate's 'rewrite SIMD to logical C++' rule); SetupLoadingScreenTexture's platform descriptor int-copy loops -> clean struct assignment + create/lock/memcpy/unlock; member offsets reconciled vs pseudocode (muLastTime@24, mbRenderInBackground@48, mfBlackOverlayFade@52, mfTimeStep@88). Gate PASS. Parity RED is (a) member-call false positives: all 8 Im2d methods ARE called via lpIm2d->X() but the qualified-name matcher misses member syntax; (b) the 2 descriptor loops correctly abstracted away; (c) __fastcall void-return artifacts. .rdata floats (fade rates, UVs) + the overlay blend state declared extern (link-time).

class:BrnPhysics::Deformation::Deformati.notes: Gate-only (self-reviewed, layout recovery). Compile PASS. Construct @0x8228F1B0 constructs 5 inline event queues + clears 2 flags. Layout recovered from the ctor's write offsets: queues at X360 bytes 116/928/2544/2880/6896 reconcile EXACTLY once JointedPartStateEvent is plain-aligned (4) not alignas(16) - its queue @116 is only 4-aligned, proving align4 (header 12, others align16 header 16); 116+812=928, +1616=2544, +336=2880, +4016=6896, +3856=10752 (the trailing flag a1[2688]). Members declared in that order + named access (no raw offsets). PC build is x64 so absolute offsets differ from X360 (pointer size) - structural parity, not byte-match, per project policy. Omitted the 5 redundant post-Construct miLength re-zeros (queue ctor already clears count). Leading region + 2 flags padded/placeholder (unrecovered).

class:CgsGui::StateInterface.notes: Complex/foundational: reconstructed the real GUI-state base hierarchy (CgsFsm::State -> ScriptedState -> CgsGui::State, CgsGui::StateInterface + GuiAccessPointers/LanguageManager/VariableEventQueue/GuiEvent vocabulary) from DecFIGS DWARF as real headers per the new workflow. Gate PASS. Parity RED is fully explained: all absent callees (CgsDev::Assert::Begin/Fire/EndAssert, BasePriorityQueue::Clear) and all 6 branches are the CGS_ASSERT no-op macro (CgsAssert.h), the project-wide established convention; return-count delta is void emitters falling off the end. 7/7 funcs faithful: accessors=assert+named-member return; emitters build the correct GuiEvent<N> record and push on the X360 channel (41/40/39) with the X360 byte size documented inline.
